content.problemStatement:
You are given two integer sequences A1,A2,вЂ¦,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1вЂ‹,A2вЂ‹,вЂ¦,ANвЂ‹ and B1,B2,вЂ¦,BNB_1, B_2, \dots, B_NB1вЂ‹,B2вЂ‹,вЂ¦,BNвЂ‹.

We define the similarity of the sequences AAA and BBB as the number of indices iii such that Ai=BiA_i = B_iAiвЂ‹=BiвЂ‹.

You should process QQQ queries. In each query, you are given parameters lll, rrr and ccc; you should change each of the elements Al,Al+1,вЂ¦,ArA_l, A_{l+1}, \dots, A_rAlвЂ‹,Al+1вЂ‹,вЂ¦,ArвЂ‹ to ccc, and then compute the similarity of the sequences AAA and BBB.

Chef knows that this is simple, so he asks you to answer the queries online. Note that the input is given in a special format to ensure that.
constraints: 1в‰¤Ai,Biв‰¤1091 \le A_i, B_i \le 10^91в‰¤AiвЂ‹,BiвЂ‹в‰¤109 for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,вЂ¦,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1вЂ‹,A2вЂ‹,вЂ¦,ANвЂ‹.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,вЂ¦,BNB_1, B_2, \dots, B_NB1вЂ‹,B2вЂ‹,вЂ¦,BNвЂ‹.
Each of the next QQQ lines contains three space-separated integers xxx, yyy and zzz describing a query in the following format:

Let's denote the similarity of AAA and BBB before this query by PrP_rPrвЂ‹ (for the first query, PrP_rPrвЂ‹ is the similarity of the initial sequences).
For this query, l=xвЉ•Prl = x \oplus P_rl=xвЉ•PrвЂ‹, r=yвЉ•Prr = y \oplus P_rr=yвЉ•PrвЂ‹ and c=zвЉ•Prc = z \oplus P_rc=zвЉ•PrвЂ‹.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing one integer вЂ” the similarity after this query.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The similarity before any query is applied is 222 (the sequences match at positions 111 and 333).
In the first query, l=2l = 2l=2, r=3r=3r=3, c=2c=2c=2. The similarity after this query is 111 (the only match is at position 111).
In the second query, l=1l = 1l=1, r=4r=4r=4, c=2c=2c=2. The similarity after this query is 000 (no match at any position).
In the third query, l=1l = 1l=1, r=4r=4r=4, c=1c=1c=1. The similarity after this query is 222 (two matches at positions 111 and 222).
